_meta.purpose: Core memory file for FORA. This is the source of truth the brainstorm prompt and code generator read from. Every fact on a deployed landing page must trace back to this file. Do not surface anything not explicitly present here.
_meta.pipeline: FORA — an agentic pipeline that reads this file, runs a narrative brainstorm per opportunity, and generates personalised application landing pages.
_meta.instructions_for_brainstorm_agent: Read this file fully before producing a content brief. When selecting which stories, signals, and stats to surface, match them to the JD signals provided. Respect the nda_sensitive and unlaunched flags — use the framing_guidance field on each case study to handle sensitive items. Never fabricate details not present in this file. For unlaunched work, prefer framing the story around the decision and process rather than the shipped outcome.
_meta.application_history_note: Application history lives in applications/applications.json — not in this file. This file is career knowledge only.
_meta.instructions_for_filling: Fill every field honestly. Vague entries produce vague pages. The more specific you are — naming the product, the decision, the constraint, the outcome — the better the generated page will be. Fields marked REQUIRED must be filled. Fields marked OPTIONAL can be left as null.

identity.name: YOUR FULL NAME
identity.title: YOUR CURRENT TITLE
identity.tagline: One sentence. Your professional point of view. Not a job title. What you believe about your work.
identity.positioning: 2–3 sentences. How you operate. What makes your career arc different from someone with the same job titles. Write in first person. Be specific about what you've built and the context you built it in.
core_skills: List your actual skills — not generic ones
Name specific methodologies, systems, or approaches
Only include what you can back up with a story
tools: List tools you actually use daily

philosophy.design_thinking: One or two sentences on how you approach design problems. Your actual view — not a textbook answer.
philosophy.on_ai: Optional. Your relationship with AI tools in your workflow.
philosophy.on_systems: Optional. Your view on design systems, if relevant to your work.
philosophy.personal_philosophy: Optional. A belief or framework that shapes how you work. Specific is better than generic.
philosophy.on_building: Optional. Your relationship with building — products, teams, processes.

career.company_description: One sentence. What the company does, its stage, and domain.
career.unlaunched_note: If unlaunched: explain why. Was it regulatory? Company decision? Still in progress? This context helps the brainstorm agent frame it correctly.
career.framing_note: Optional. Specific instructions to the brainstorm agent on how to present this role. Use this for nuance that isn't captured in the structured fields.
career.headline_story: 2–3 sentences. The most important thing about this role. What you walked into, what you built, what changed.

key_decisions.rationale: Why you made it — the constraint, the insight, the trade-off

what_was_built: Name the product, system, or process
career.key_insight: The single most important thing you learned or proved in this role.

case_studies.title: Case Study Title
case_studies.display_status: Shipped — fully presentable | Presentable — focus on process and decisions | NDA — internal use only
case_studies.framing_guidance: Instructions to the brainstorm agent on how to present this case study. What to lead with. What to avoid. How to handle sensitive elements.
case_studies.problem: What problem were you solving? Be specific about who had the problem and why it mattered.
case_studies.insight: The key insight that changed how you approached the problem.
case_studies.outcome: What shipped or changed. Specific. Numbers if you have them.
key_decisions: A decision that defined the outcome

signal_name.description: What this signal says about you as a designer. One sentence.
evidence: Specific evidence from your career that proves this signal
Name the company, product, or decision
best_for_roles: Types of roles where this signal is most relevant
The brainstorm agent uses this to decide which signals to surface per JD

current_projects.name: Project Name
current_projects.description: 2–3 sentences. What it is, what problem it solves, what makes it interesting.
current_projects.stage: More detail on where it is and what's next.

interests: List genuine interests — these add texture when relevant
personal.philosophy_anchor: Optional. A philosophy, framework, or belief system that shapes how you work. Specific is better.
aesthetic_references: References that inform your visual and design aesthetic
personal.portfolio_aesthetic: Describe the visual feel of your portfolio or personal brand. Fonts, colours, mood. This informs the default design system.

rules: List rules for how copy about you should be written
What to avoid — filler, clichés, vague claims
What to use — specific language, sentence length, tone
do: Name the product and the decision
